# @meli/statsd

Send custom metrics from an application to [**Datadog**](https://app.datadoghq.com/account/login/id/c2Tgu3) using the [StatsD standard](https://thenewstack.io/collecting-metrics-using-statsd-a-standard-for-real-time-monitoring/).

## Description

`@meli/statsd` provides a pre-configured `StatsD` wrapper, from [hot-shots](https://github.com/bdeitte/hot-shots), to send metrics to Datadog.

The module automatically detects your environment and behaves accordingly:

- **Production FURY environment**: Metrics are sent to Datadog via StatsD with buffering enabled
- **Development and testing**: Metrics are NOT sent, for performance and safety

## Install

```sh
npm install --save @meli/statsd
```

## Usage

### Quick Start

After installation, create a MeliStatsD instance and start tracking metrics:

```js
import { MeliStatsD } from '@meli/statsd';

const statsd = new MeliStatsD();

// Track a simple counter
statsd.increment('page.views');

// Track a gauge with value
statsd.gauge('memory.usage', 85.5);

// Track timing
const start = Date.now();
await doSomething();
const duration = Date.now() - start;
statsd.timing('operation.duration', duration);
```

### `MeliStatsD` Class

The main class MeliStatsD to instantiate a client for sending custom metrics.

#### `MeliStatsD` Initialisation

```js
import { MeliStatsD } from '@meli/statsd';

const statsd = new MeliStatsD();
const statsd = new MeliStatsD(options);
```

##### `MeliStatsD` Initialisation Options

None of them are required.

| Property     |   Type   | Description                                                  |
| ------------ | :------: | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `prefix`     | `string` | Prefix to add to all metric names. Defaults to empty string. |
| `suffix`     | `string` | Suffix to add to all metric names. Defaults to empty string. |
| `globalTags` | `object` | Tags to add to all metrics. Defaults to empty object.        |

#### Available Methods

All methods from the underlying `StatsD` client are available. Here are the most commonly used ones:

> [!TIP]
>
> For complete method documentation and additional options, refer to the [hot-shots StatsD documentation](https://github.com/bdeitte/hot-shots?tab=readme-ov-file#statsd-methods).

##### `statsd.increment()`

Increment a counter metric.

```js
statsd.increment('page.views');
statsd.increment('page.views', 1, 0.1); // increment by 1 with 10% sample rate
statsd.increment('page.views', 1, 0.1, { user: 'premium' }); // with tags
```

##### `statsd.decrement()`

Decrement a counter metric.

```js
statsd.decrement('active.users');
statsd.decrement('active.users', 1, 0.1, { region: 'us-east' });
```

##### `statsd.gauge()`

Set a gauge metric value.

```js
statsd.gauge('memory.usage', 85.5);
statsd.gauge('temperature', 23.5, 1.0, { sensor: 'room1' });
```

##### `statsd.histogram()`

Send a histogram metric.

```js
statsd.histogram('response.size', 1024);
statsd.histogram('response.size', 1024, 1.0, { endpoint: '/api/users' });
```

##### `statsd.timing()`

Send a timing metric.

```js
const start = Date.now();
await doSomething();
const duration = Date.now() - start;
statsd.timing('operation.duration', duration);
```

##### `statsd.set()`

Send a set metric.

```js
statsd.set('unique.users', 'user123');
statsd.set('unique.users', 'user123', 1.0, { plan: 'premium' });
```

##### `statsd.unique()`

Send a unique metric.

```js
statsd.unique('unique.visitors', 'visitor456');
```

## Examples

### Basic Usage

```js
import { MeliStatsD } from '@meli/statsd';

const statsd = new MeliStatsD();

// Track page views
statsd.increment('page.views');

// Track memory usage
statsd.gauge('memory.usage', process.memoryUsage().heapUsed / 1024 / 1024);

// Track response time
const start = Date.now();
await processRequest();
const duration = Date.now() - start;
statsd.timing('request.duration', duration);
```

### Custom Client with Tags

```js
import { MeliStatsD } from '@meli/statsd';

const statsd = new MeliStatsD({
  prefix: 'myapp.',
  suffix: '.v1',
  globalTags: {
    environment: 'production',
    region: 'us-east-1',
  },
});

// All metrics will be prefixed with 'myapp.' and suffixed with '.v1'
// All metrics will include the global tags
statsd.increment('user.login'); // Becomes 'myapp.user.login.v1' with tags
```
